title: Question 1.1
statement_text: For which $\varepsilon>0$ and $k>0$ does there exist a bound $c$ such that each simple graph $G$ either contains $C_{2k+1}$ as a subgraph or $\chi(G)\leq c$ (or both)?
context_text: Erdős and Simonovits (1972) asked whether every graph with no fixed odd cycle and large minimum degree has bounded chromatic number, specifically for triangles and $C_5$. The paper authors present Question 1.1 as a generalization of those questions. Thomassen subsequently answered it affirmatively for $C_3$ at $\varepsilon>1/3$ and for all longer odd cycles at all $\varepsilon>0$.
notes: Presented as background context; the specific cases addressed by Thomassen [11,12] are resolved, but the fully general formulation is posed by the paper authors as a unifying question.
paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

title: Question 1.2
statement_text: Which orientations of cycles are contained in every digraph with high minimum out-degree and high chromatic number?
context_text: This is the central question of the paper, investigating a directed analogue of the chromatic threshold for cycles. The authors consider digraphs $D$ without loops or parallel arcs with minimum out-degree at least $\varepsilon|V(D)|$ for fixed $\varepsilon>0$. Theorem 1.3 in this paper fully answers Question 1.2.
notes: This question is fully resolved within the same paper by Theorem 1.3; included because it is a labelled question environment and the central research question.
paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

title: Question 4.1
statement_text: For which fixed digraphs $H$ does there exist a constant $c_{\varepsilon}\geq 1$ for all $\varepsilon>0$ such that every digraph $D$ without loops or parallel arcs with $\chi(D)\geq c_{\varepsilon}$ and minimum out-degree at least $\varepsilon|V(D)|$ contains $H$ as a subdigraph?
context_text: This is a more general version of Question 1.2, replacing cycle orientations by arbitrary fixed digraphs $H$. Theorem 1.3 characterizes the orientations of cycles for which this question is answered affirmatively. The authors also note that all subdigraphs of directed complete bipartite digraphs (with uniform arc orientation between parts) give an affirmative answer via a Ramsey-type argument.
paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

title: Question 4.2
statement_text: For a fixed digraph $H$, what is the minimum value $\varepsilon_{H}\geq 0$ such that there exists a constant $c\geq 1$ such that every digraph $D$ without loops or parallel arcs with $\chi(D)\geq c$ and minimum out-degree at least $\varepsilon_{H}|V(D)|$ contains $H$ as a subdigraph?
context_text: The quantity $\varepsilon_H$ provides a directed analogue of the chromatic threshold studied for undirected graphs. Theorem 1.3 may be interpreted as characterizing the orientations of cycles for which the directed chromatic threshold $\varepsilon_H$ equals zero. The authors also suggest studying variants where high chromatic number is replaced by high dichromatic number.
notes: The authors note that $\varepsilon_H = 1$ is always an upper bound, so the quantity is well-defined for all digraphs $H$.
paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree
